title: LLDModule shell + mode selector
description: Create the top-level LLDModule component with a mode selector supporting Class Diagram, Sequence Diagram, State Machine, Design Patterns, SOLID Principles, and LLD Problems modes.
acceptanceCriteria: LLDModule renders with a tab/selector for all 6 modes (Class, Sequence, State, Patterns, SOLID, Problems)
Selecting a mode lazy-loads the corresponding sub-module
Active mode persists in URL query parameter for shareability
Module registered in the app router and accessible from navigation

title: LLD type definitions
description: Define all TypeScript types for LLD: UMLClass (name, attributes, methods, visibility), UMLRelationship (type, source, target, cardinality), SequenceMessage, StateMachine, and DesignPattern.
acceptanceCriteria: UMLClass type includes name, stereotype, attributes (with visibility/type), and methods (with params/return)
UMLRelationship type covers all 6 relationship kinds with source/target handles and cardinality labels
SequenceMessage, StateMachine, and DesignPattern types are fully defined with discriminated unions where appropriate

title: LLD Zustand store
description: Create the Zustand store for LLD module managing active diagram type, classes array, relationships array, code editor content, sync direction flag, and selected pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Store exposes slices for classes, relationships, sequences, states, and patterns
Active diagram type selector updates the rendered canvas
Code editor content slice supports get/set with sync direction flag
All mutations are immutable and support undo/redo via temporal middleware

title: ClassNode (3-section UML box)
description: Build a React Flow custom node rendering a proper UML class box with three sections: class name (with stereotype), attributes (with visibility icons +/-/#/~), and methods (with signatures).
acceptanceCriteria: Node renders three distinct horizontal sections separated by dividers
Class name section shows stereotype (<<interface>>, <<abstract>>) when applicable
Attributes display visibility prefix (+public, -private, #protected, ~package) and type annotations
Methods display visibility, parameter list, and return type

title: 6 relationship edge types
description: Implement 6 custom React Flow edge types for UML class diagrams: inheritance (hollow triangle), composition (filled diamond), aggregation (hollow diamond), association (arrow), dependency (dashed arrow), and realization (dashed + hollow triangle).
acceptanceCriteria: Each edge type renders the correct SVG marker (triangle, diamond, arrow) at the target end
Dashed line styles apply correctly to dependency and realization edges
Edges support optional cardinality labels (1, 0..*, 1..*) at each end
Edge type is selectable from a context menu on connection creation

title: LLD drag-drop palette
description: Create a sidebar palette with draggable items for Class, Interface, Abstract Class, Enum, and Package that can be dropped onto the React Flow canvas to create new nodes.
acceptanceCriteria: Palette displays 5 draggable items with UML-appropriate icons
Dragging an item onto the canvas creates the corresponding node at the drop position
New nodes are initialized with placeholder name and empty attributes/methods
Package nodes act as group containers that can hold other class nodes

title: Class diagram model (add/remove/edit, validation)
description: Implement the core class diagram model with CRUD operations for classes and relationships, including validation rules (no duplicate class names, valid relationship targets, no self-inheritance).
acceptanceCriteria: addClass, removeClass, updateClass functions operate immutably on the store
addRelationship validates that source/target exist and prevents invalid combinations
Duplicate class name detection warns or prevents conflicts
Removing a class cascades removal of connected relationships

title: Class properties panel
description: Build a side panel for editing the selected class node's attributes (name, type, visibility), methods (name, params, return type, visibility), and relationship cardinality.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel appears when a class node is selected and shows all editable fields
Attributes and methods can be added, removed, and reordered via drag handles
Visibility can be toggled via dropdown (public, private, protected, package)
Changes update the store and re-render the ClassNode in real time

title: Sequence diagram custom canvas
description: Build a custom canvas for sequence diagrams with vertical lifelines, activation bars (stacked for nested calls), and self-call loops, rendered on a React Flow pane.
acceptanceCriteria: Lifeline nodes render as named rectangles with dashed vertical lines extending downward
Activation bars appear on lifelines during method execution and stack for nested calls
Self-call messages render as a loopback arrow on the same lifeline
Canvas supports vertical auto-layout with configurable spacing between messages

title: Sequence message edges
description: Implement 4 sequence message edge types: synchronous (solid arrow, filled head), asynchronous (solid arrow, open head), return (dashed arrow, open head), and self-call (loopback).
acceptanceCriteria: Sync messages render as solid lines with filled arrowheads
Async messages render as solid lines with open arrowheads
Return messages render as dashed lines with open arrowheads
All message edges display a label for the method name and arguments

title: Combined fragments (alt, opt, loop, par, break, critical)
description: Implement combined fragment overlays for sequence diagrams supporting alt (if/else), opt (optional), loop (iteration), par (parallel), break, and critical interaction operators.
acceptanceCriteria: Fragments render as labeled rectangles spanning involved lifelines with operator tag in top-left
Alt fragments support multiple operand sections separated by dashed lines
Loop fragments display guard condition text (e.g., [i < n])
Fragments can be nested and properly z-ordered

title: State machine nodes
description: Implement custom React Flow nodes for state machine diagrams: regular state (rounded rectangle), composite state (with nested region), initial pseudostate (filled circle), final state (bullseye), and history pseudostate (H/H*).
acceptanceCriteria: State nodes render as rounded rectangles with optional entry/do/exit action compartments
Composite state nodes contain a sub-region that can hold child states
Initial pseudostate renders as a small filled circle, final state as a bullseye
History pseudostates render as circled H (shallow) or H* (deep)

title: State machine transitions with guards/actions
description: Implement state machine transition edges that display event triggers, guard conditions (in square brackets), and actions (after slash), following UML notation: event[guard]/action.
acceptanceCriteria: Transition edges display labels in the format event[guard]/action
Guard conditions are optional and render in square brackets
Self-transitions render as loopback edges on the same state node
Transitions support multi-segment routing to avoid overlapping labels

title: Diagram-to-TypeScript code generation
description: Implement a code generator that converts the class diagram model into TypeScript source code with classes, interfaces, enums, inheritance, and typed properties/methods.
acceptanceCriteria: Classes generate with correct extends/implements keywords based on relationship types
Attributes generate as typed properties with visibility modifiers (public/private/protected)
Methods generate with parameter types and return types
Generated code is syntactically valid TypeScript that passes a basic parse check

title: Diagram-to-Python code generation
description: Implement a code generator that converts the class diagram model into Python source code using dataclasses, ABC, Enum, and type hints.
acceptanceCriteria: Classes generate using @dataclass with typed fields and __init__ where needed
Abstract classes use ABC and @abstractmethod decorators
Enums generate using Python Enum class
Inheritance maps to Python class(ParentClass) syntax with proper MRO ordering

title: Diagram-to-Java code generation
description: Implement a code generator that converts the class diagram model into Java source code with classes, interfaces, enums, access modifiers, and proper import statements.
acceptanceCriteria: Classes generate with correct access modifiers, extends, and implements keywords
Interfaces generate with method signatures only (no bodies)
Enums generate with constant values and optional fields/constructors
Each class generates as a separate logical unit with proper package/import structure

title: Code-to-diagram parser (regex-based for TypeScript)
description: Implement a regex-based parser that extracts class definitions, interfaces, enums, properties, methods, and inheritance from TypeScript source code and produces the diagram model.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser extracts class/interface/enum declarations with their names and stereotypes
Properties parsed with visibility (public/private/protected), name, and type
Methods parsed with visibility, name, parameters, and return type
Inheritance (extends) and implementation (implements) produce corresponding relationships

title: Bidirectional sync wiring (debounced, syncDirection flag)
description: Wire up bidirectional synchronization between the code editor and class diagram canvas using a debounced update mechanism and a syncDirection flag to prevent infinite loops.
acceptanceCriteria: Editing the diagram updates the code editor after a 500ms debounce
Editing the code updates the diagram after a 500ms debounce
syncDirection flag prevents circular updates (diagram->code does not trigger code->diagram)
Sync errors display a non-blocking toast with the parse error details

title: 5 Creational pattern templates
description: Create pre-built class diagram templates for 5 creational design patterns: Singleton, Factory Method, Abstract Factory, Builder, and Prototype, each with annotated nodes and relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pattern template loads a pre-configured class diagram with properly named and typed classes
Relationships between classes use correct UML types (e.g., Factory uses dependency, Builder uses composition)
Each template includes a description panel explaining intent, participants, and when to use
Templates include example code snippets in TypeScript alongside the diagram

title: 7 Structural pattern templates
description: Create pre-built class diagram templates for 7 structural design patterns: Adapter, Bridge, Composite, Decorator, Facade, Flyweight, and Proxy.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pattern template contains correct class hierarchy and relationship types
Adapter pattern shows both class and object adapter variants
Composite pattern demonstrates the recursive tree structure with Component/Leaf/Composite
Each template includes description, participants, and TypeScript code sample

title: 11 Behavioral pattern templates
description: Create pre-built class diagram templates for 11 behavioral design patterns: Chain of Responsibility, Command, Iterator, Mediator, Memento, Observer, State, Strategy, Template Method, Visitor, and Interpreter.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pattern template contains correct class hierarchy and relationship types
Observer pattern shows Subject, Observer interface, and ConcreteObserver with subscribe/notify flow
Strategy pattern distinguishes Context, Strategy interface, and ConcreteStrategy classes
Each template includes description, participants, and TypeScript code sample

title: 10 Modern pattern templates
description: Create pre-built class diagram templates for 10 modern design patterns: Repository, CQRS, Event Sourcing, Circuit Breaker, Saga, Specification, Unit of Work, Dependency Injection Container, Service Locator, and Mediator (MediatR-style).
acceptanceCriteria: CQRS template shows separate Command and Query models with their respective handlers
Event Sourcing template includes Event Store, Aggregate, and Event types
Circuit Breaker template shows Closed/Open/HalfOpen states with transition logic
Each template includes description, use cases, and TypeScript code sample

title: Pattern browser + detail view with code toggle
description: Build a pattern browser UI that lists all design patterns grouped by category (Creational, Structural, Behavioral, Modern) with a detail view showing the diagram and a toggle between diagram and code views.
acceptanceCriteria: Pattern browser displays a filterable/searchable list grouped by category
Selecting a pattern loads its diagram template onto the canvas
Code toggle switches between the visual diagram and the generated source code
Detail view includes pattern description, participants, pros/cons, and real-world examples

title: SRP interactive demo (drag methods between classes)
description: Build an interactive Single Responsibility Principle demo where users drag methods between a monolithic class and two focused classes, with real-time SRP violation scoring.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial state shows a God class with 8+ methods spanning two responsibilities
Users can drag-and-drop methods between the God class and two target classes
A real-time SRP score indicator shows violation level (red/yellow/green)
Completion state validates correct method distribution and shows explanation

title: OCP interactive demo
description: Build an interactive Open/Closed Principle demo where users refactor a class with switch/case statements into a polymorphic design using strategy or template method pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial state shows a class with a switch statement over type discriminators
Users choose an extension approach (new subclass, new strategy) and configure it
Demo validates that the original class remains unmodified (closed for modification)
Side-by-side before/after code view highlights the structural improvement

title: LSP interactive demo
description: Build an interactive Liskov Substitution Principle demo showing the classic Rectangle/Square problem and letting users test substitutability by running assertions against substituted instances.
acceptanceCriteria: Demo presents a Rectangle base class and Square subclass with the classic violation
Users can run a test suite that calls setWidth/setHeight and checks area, revealing the violation
A refactored solution is available showing proper LSP-compliant design
Explanation panel describes behavioral subtyping and contract preservation

title: ISP interactive demo
description: Build an interactive Interface Segregation Principle demo where users split a fat interface into smaller role-specific interfaces and see which classes no longer need dummy implementations.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial state shows a fat interface with 8+ methods and 3 implementing classes with dummy no-op methods
Users drag methods to create smaller focused interfaces
Implementing classes auto-update to only implement relevant interfaces
Completion state highlights eliminated dummy methods and shows the clean design

title: DIP interactive demo
description: Build an interactive Dependency Inversion Principle demo where users refactor direct class dependencies into abstractions by introducing interfaces between high-level and low-level modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial state shows a high-level module directly depending on low-level concrete classes
Users create abstraction interfaces and wire dependencies through them
Dependency arrows visually flip direction (both point toward abstractions) upon correct refactoring
Demo validates that high-level module only imports abstractions, not concretions

title: 20 LLD problem definitions + browser
description: Define 20 LLD interview problems (e.g., Parking Lot, LRU Cache, Elevator System, Vending Machine, etc.) with problem statement, requirements, constraints, and expected class diagram, plus a browser UI to select and view them.
acceptanceCriteria: 20 problems defined with title, description, functional/non-functional requirements, and constraints
Problem browser displays problems in a searchable card grid with difficulty tags
Selecting a problem loads its requirements into a side panel alongside an empty canvas
Each problem has a difficulty rating (Easy/Medium/Hard) and estimated time

title: LLD problem starter templates + solutions
description: Create starter templates (partial class diagrams with key classes) and full solution diagrams for all 20 LLD problems, with a reveal mechanism that progressively shows the solution.
acceptanceCriteria: Each problem has a starter template with 2-3 key classes pre-placed on the canvas
Full solution diagrams include all classes, relationships, and method signatures
Progressive reveal mechanism shows solution one class at a time with explanation
Users can compare their diagram against the solution with a diff overlay

title: DatabaseModule shell + mode selector
description: Create the top-level DatabaseModule component with a mode selector supporting ER Diagram, Normalization, Query Plan, Index Visualization, and Transaction Isolation modes.
acceptanceCriteria: DatabaseModule renders with a tab/selector for all 5 modes
Selecting a mode lazy-loads the corresponding sub-module
Active mode persists in URL query parameter for shareability
Module registered in the app router and accessible from navigation

title: Database type definitions
description: Define all TypeScript types for the database module: EREntity (attributes, keys), ERRelationship (cardinality, participation), FunctionalDependency, NormalForm, and QueryPlanNode.
acceptanceCriteria: EREntity type includes name, attributes with key/type annotations, and weak entity flag
ERRelationship type covers cardinality (1:1, 1:N, M:N) and participation (total/partial)
FunctionalDependency type represents determinant and dependent attribute sets
QueryPlanNode type supports nested tree structure with cost/rows estimates

title: Database Zustand store
description: Create the Zustand store for the database module managing ER entities, relationships, functional dependencies, normalization state, query plans, and index configurations.
acceptanceCriteria: Store exposes slices for ER diagram, normalization, query plans, and indexes
ER diagram slice manages entities and relationships with CRUD operations
Normalization slice tracks functional dependencies, candidate keys, and current normal form
All mutations are immutable and support undo/redo

title: EntityNode (Chen notation with attributes)
description: Build a custom React Flow node for ER entities in Chen notation: rectangle for entity name with elliptical attribute nodes connected by lines, including primary key (underlined) and multivalued (double ellipse) attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: Entity renders as a labeled rectangle in Chen notation style
Attributes render as ellipses connected to the entity by lines
Primary key attributes display with underlined text
Multivalued attributes render with a double ellipse border

title: WeakEntityNode (double border)
description: Build a custom React Flow node for weak entities in Chen notation rendered with a double-border rectangle, with its identifying relationship and partial key attribute (dashed underline).
acceptanceCriteria: Weak entity renders as a double-bordered rectangle
Partial key attributes display with dashed underlines
Weak entity connects to its identifying (strong) entity via a double-bordered diamond
Total participation is shown by double lines from weak entity to identifying relationship

title: RelationshipDiamond (Chen notation)
description: Build a custom React Flow node for ER relationships in Chen notation rendered as a diamond shape with the relationship name centered inside.
acceptanceCriteria: Relationship renders as a diamond (rotated square) with centered label text
Diamond supports both single and double border (for identifying relationships)
Relationship attributes can be attached as ellipses similar to entity attributes
Node handles are positioned at the four diamond points for edge connections

title: ERRelationshipEdge with cardinality + participation
description: Build a custom React Flow edge for ER relationships that displays cardinality labels (1, N, M) near the entity ends and uses single or double lines to indicate partial or total participation.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge displays cardinality labels (1, N, M) positioned near each connected entity
Single line indicates partial participation, double line indicates total participation
Edge connects entities to relationship diamonds in Chen notation
Labels and line styles update reactively when the underlying model changes

title: CrowsFootEdge implementation
description: Build a custom React Flow edge for Crow's Foot notation that renders proper end markers: circle (zero), dash (one), crow's foot (many), and their combinations (zero-or-many, one-or-many, exactly-one, zero-or-one).
acceptanceCriteria: Edge renders correct Crow's Foot markers at both source and target ends
All standard combinations supported: ||--||, ||--|{, ||--o{, |o--o{, etc.
SVG markers render crisply at all zoom levels
Edge type is selectable from a dropdown when creating connections

title: Chen to Crow's Foot notation toggle
description: Implement a notation toggle that converts the ER diagram between Chen notation (entities + diamonds + attribute ellipses) and Crow's Foot notation (table boxes with column lists), preserving all semantic information.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle button switches the entire diagram between Chen and Crow's Foot rendering
Chen-to-Crow's-Foot maps entities to table boxes and collapses relationship diamonds into edges
Crow's-Foot-to-Chen expands edges back to diamond nodes with attribute ellipses
All cardinality and participation constraints are preserved across notation switches

title: ER diagram palette
description: Create a sidebar palette with draggable items for Entity, Weak Entity, Relationship, Attribute (regular, key, multivalued, derived, composite) that can be dropped onto the ER diagram canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Palette displays draggable items for all ER element types with Chen notation icons
Dragging an item onto the canvas creates the corresponding node at the drop position
Attribute items auto-connect to the nearest entity when dropped near one
Palette adapts to the active notation mode (Chen items for Chen mode, table elements for Crow's Foot)

title: ER-to-relational schema conversion
description: Implement an algorithm that converts an ER diagram into a relational schema, handling strong entities, weak entities, 1:1/1:N/M:N relationships, multivalued attributes, and generating foreign key constraints.
acceptanceCriteria: Strong entities map to tables with primary keys correctly
1:N relationships add foreign key to the N-side table
M:N relationships create a junction table with composite primary key
Weak entities include the owner's primary key as part of their composite key

title: Normalization: attribute closure + candidate key finder
description: Implement algorithms for computing the attribute closure of a set of attributes under a given set of functional dependencies, and finding all candidate keys of a relation.
acceptanceCriteria: Attribute closure algorithm correctly computes X+ given X and a set of FDs
Candidate key finder identifies all minimal superkeys
Algorithm handles trivial FDs, transitive dependencies, and augmentation
Step-by-step trace available showing which FDs were applied at each step

title: Normalization: normal form detection + decomposition
description: Implement algorithms that detect the current normal form (1NF through BCNF) of a relation and perform lossless-join decomposition to achieve BCNF, with dependency preservation analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal form detector correctly identifies 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF violations with explanations
BCNF decomposition algorithm produces a lossless-join decomposition
Algorithm reports which functional dependencies are lost during decomposition
Each decomposition step shows the violating FD and the resulting sub-relations

title: NormalizationStepper UI
description: Build a step-through UI for the normalization process showing the relation at each step, the violating FD, and the resulting decomposition, with forward/backward navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Stepper shows the current relation with its attributes and FDs at each step
Violating FD is highlighted in red with an explanation of why it violates the target NF
Forward/backward buttons navigate through decomposition steps
Final step shows all resulting relations with their keys and normal form levels

title: Query plan parser (PostgreSQL EXPLAIN JSON)
description: Implement a parser that takes PostgreSQL EXPLAIN (FORMAT JSON) output and converts it into a tree of QueryPlanNode objects with operator type, cost, rows, width, and children.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser correctly handles nested plan nodes from EXPLAIN JSON output
Each node extracts operator type, startup cost, total cost, actual rows, and width
Parser supports common operators: Seq Scan, Index Scan, Hash Join, Merge Join, Nested Loop, Sort, Aggregate
Invalid or malformed JSON input returns a descriptive parse error

title: Query plan heuristic simulator (SQL to plan tree)
description: Implement a heuristic-based query plan simulator that takes a simplified SQL query and produces a plan tree by applying heuristic optimization rules (push selections down, join ordering).
acceptanceCriteria: Simulator parses simplified SELECT/FROM/WHERE/JOIN SQL queries
Heuristic rules applied: push selections below joins, push projections down, convert selections to index scans when index available
Produces a plan tree with estimated costs based on table statistics (configurable row counts)
Step-by-step trace shows each heuristic rule application and the resulting tree transformation

title: QueryPlanTree visualization
description: Build a tree visualization component that renders a query plan as a top-down tree with operator nodes showing type, cost, and row estimates, with color-coding for expensive operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Plan tree renders as a top-down tree with nodes for each operator
Each node displays operator type, estimated cost, and actual/estimated rows
Nodes are color-coded by cost relative to total query cost (green to red gradient)
Clicking a node shows detailed properties (filter conditions, index name, join type)

title: B-Tree index visualization
description: Build an interactive B-Tree visualization that supports insert, delete, and search operations with animated node splits, merges, and key redistributions.
acceptanceCriteria: B-Tree renders as a balanced tree with configurable order (default m=4)
Insert operation animates key placement and node splits when overflow occurs
Search operation highlights the traversal path from root to the target key
Delete operation shows key redistribution and node merge animations

title: B+ Tree index visualization
description: Build an interactive B+ Tree visualization with leaf-level linked list, showing insert, delete, search, and range query operations with animations.
acceptanceCriteria: B+ Tree renders with internal nodes (keys only) and leaf nodes (keys + values) connected by a linked list
Insert operation animates leaf splits and key promotion to parent
Range query operation highlights the start leaf and follows the linked list to the end
Visual distinction between internal nodes and leaf nodes is clear

title: Hash index visualization
description: Build an interactive hash index visualization supporting static and extendible hashing, showing bucket allocation, overflow chains, and rehashing/splitting.
acceptanceCriteria: Static hashing shows a fixed bucket array with overflow chains for collisions
Extendible hashing shows global depth, local depth, directory, and bucket pointers
Insert operation animates hash computation, bucket placement, and split when overflow occurs
Search operation highlights hash computation and bucket lookup path

title: Transaction isolation simulator (2-transaction model)
description: Implement a transaction isolation simulator that models two concurrent transactions executing interleaved operations on a shared database, demonstrating isolation level effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulator supports all 4 isolation levels: Read Uncommitted, Read Committed, Repeatable Read, Serializable
Two transactions can be configured with read/write operations on shared rows
Execution model simulates interleaved scheduling with lock/MVCC semantics
Simulator correctly produces or prevents anomalies based on the selected isolation level

title: TransactionIsolationDemo UI
description: Build a UI for the transaction isolation simulator showing two parallel transaction timelines, shared data state, and demonstrations of dirty read, non-repeatable read, and phantom read anomalies.
acceptanceCriteria: Two parallel vertical timelines show operations for Transaction A and Transaction B
Shared data panel shows the current database state and any uncommitted changes
Isolation level selector switches behavior and highlights which anomalies are possible
Step-by-step playback with play/pause/step controls and anomaly callout annotations

title: FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, Priority scheduling algorithms
description: Implement core CPU scheduling algorithms: First Come First Served, Shortest Job First (preemptive and non-preemptive), Round Robin with configurable quantum, and Priority Scheduling.
acceptanceCriteria: All four scheduling algorithms produce correct process execution order and timing
Round Robin supports configurable time quantum
Each algorithm computes turnaround time, waiting time, and response time per process

title: FIFO, LRU, Optimal, Clock page replacement algorithms
description: Implement page replacement algorithms: FIFO, LRU, Optimal, and Clock (Second Chance), each producing a step-by-step trace of page faults and frame state.
acceptanceCriteria: All four algorithms produce correct page fault counts for given reference strings
Step-by-step trace shows frame contents after each page reference
Clock algorithm correctly tracks reference bits and the clock hand position

title: Deadlock detection (RAG cycle detection) + Banker's Algorithm
description: Implement deadlock detection via Resource Allocation Graph cycle detection and Banker's Algorithm for deadlock avoidance with safety sequence computation.
acceptanceCriteria: RAG cycle detection identifies deadlock in resource allocation graphs
Banker's Algorithm correctly determines safe/unsafe states
Safety sequence computation produces a valid ordering when the state is safe

title: Virtual memory (address translation, TLB)
description: Implement virtual memory simulation with page table-based address translation, TLB caching, and page fault handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Address translation correctly splits virtual address into page number and offset
TLB lookup, miss, and update logic implemented correctly
Page fault handling triggers page replacement algorithm integration

title: OSModule with CPU Scheduling + Page Replacement visualizations
description: Create the OSModule shell component with CPU Scheduling and Page Replacement visualization sub-modules wired up and functional.
acceptanceCriteria: OSModule renders with tab selector for CPU Scheduling and Page Replacement modes
CPU Scheduling mode allows process input and algorithm selection with visual output
Page Replacement mode allows reference string input and frame count configuration

title: Gantt chart renderer + process metrics
description: Build a Gantt chart component that visualizes CPU scheduling results with colored process blocks on a timeline, plus a metrics table showing turnaround, waiting, and response times.
acceptanceCriteria: Gantt chart renders colored blocks per process on a horizontal timeline
Metrics table displays turnaround, waiting, and response time per process plus averages
Idle CPU time shown as distinct gray blocks on the chart

title: MLFQ scheduling algorithm implementation
description: Implement the Multi-Level Feedback Queue scheduling algorithm with configurable queue count, per-queue time quanta, priority boost interval, and demotion rules.
acceptanceCriteria: MLFQ supports configurable number of queues (default 3) with decreasing priority and increasing time quantum
Processes demote to a lower queue when they exhaust their quantum at the current level
Priority boost moves all processes to the highest queue at a configurable interval
Algorithm produces correct execution trace with queue level transitions per process

title: Deadlock Detection visualization (RAG with cycle highlighting)
description: Build a visualization for Resource Allocation Graphs that renders processes and resources as nodes with request/assignment edges, and highlights detected cycles in red to indicate deadlock.
acceptanceCriteria: Process nodes render as circles and resource nodes as rectangles with instance dots
Request edges (process-to-resource) and assignment edges (resource-to-process) render with distinct arrow styles
Cycle detection highlights all edges and nodes in the deadlock cycle in red
Users can add/remove processes, resources, and edges interactively

title: Banker's Algorithm step-through UI
description: Build a step-through UI for Banker's Algorithm showing the Allocation, Max, Need, and Available matrices, the Work vector, and the safe sequence being built step by step.
acceptanceCriteria: UI displays Allocation, Max, Need, and Available matrices in a clear tabular format
Step-through shows the Work vector updating and each process being selected for the safe sequence
Unsafe states clearly indicate which processes cannot proceed and why
Users can edit the matrices and re-run the algorithm interactively

title: Memory Management visualization (paging, TLB, page faults)
description: Build a memory management visualization showing the virtual-to-physical address translation pipeline with page table, TLB, and physical memory frames, animating TLB hits/misses and page faults.
acceptanceCriteria: Visualization shows virtual address breakdown (page number + offset) with animated bit splitting
TLB lookup animates with hit (green) or miss (red) highlighting
Page table shows entries with valid/invalid bits and frame numbers
Page fault triggers a page replacement animation showing the evicted and loaded pages

title: Thread synchronization primitives visualization
description: Build interactive visualizations for thread synchronization primitives: mutex (lock/unlock), semaphore (wait/signal with counter), monitor (condition variables), and reader-writer lock.
acceptanceCriteria: Mutex visualization shows threads competing for a lock with one in critical section and others waiting
Semaphore visualization shows the counter value and waiting queue as threads call wait/signal
Monitor visualization shows condition variable wait queue and signaling mechanism
Reader-writer lock shows concurrent readers and exclusive writer access patterns

title: Scheduling comparison mode (side-by-side all algorithms)
description: Build a comparison mode that runs the same process set through all scheduling algorithms simultaneously and displays Gantt charts and metrics side by side for easy comparison.
acceptanceCriteria: Same process input runs through FCFS, SJF, Round Robin, Priority, and MLFQ simultaneously
Side-by-side Gantt charts render with synchronized time axes
Comparison metrics table shows average turnaround, waiting, and response times for each algorithm
Highlight the best-performing algorithm for each metric

title: Belady's anomaly demonstration
description: Build an interactive demonstration of Belady's anomaly showing that increasing the number of frames can increase page faults under FIFO, with a comparison against LRU which does not exhibit the anomaly.
acceptanceCriteria: Demo uses the classic Belady's anomaly reference string (or allows custom input)
Runs FIFO with N and N+1 frames, showing page fault counts for each
Visual comparison highlights the case where more frames cause more faults
Side comparison with LRU proves LRU is a stack algorithm immune to the anomaly

title: Sleeping Barber animated demo
description: Build an animated simulation of the Sleeping Barber problem showing the barber, waiting chairs, customers arriving, and synchronization via semaphores/mutex.
acceptanceCriteria: Animation shows a barber, N waiting chairs, and customers arriving at random intervals
Barber sleeps when no customers are waiting and wakes up when a customer arrives
Customers leave if all waiting chairs are full (lost customer counter)
Semaphore and mutex states displayed alongside the animation showing synchronization

title: Readers-Writers animated demo
description: Build an animated simulation of the Readers-Writers problem showing multiple readers and writers contending for a shared resource, with configurable priority policy (reader-priority vs writer-priority).
acceptanceCriteria: Animation shows readers and writers as distinct entities approaching a shared resource
Reader-priority mode allows concurrent readers but blocks writers until all readers finish
Writer-priority mode blocks new readers when a writer is waiting
Semaphore/lock state panel shows read count, write lock, and waiting queues

title: Race condition demo (unsafe vs safe increment)
description: Build a race condition demo showing an unsafe shared counter increment by simulated threads, then a safe version using atomic operations or locks.
acceptanceCriteria: Unsafe demo shows counter values diverging from expected due to race conditions
Safe demo shows correct counter value using synchronized access
Visual timeline shows interleaved read-modify-write operations

title: Producer-Consumer bounded buffer simulation
description: Build a simulation of the Producer-Consumer problem with a bounded buffer, showing producers adding items and consumers removing them with proper synchronization.
acceptanceCriteria: Bounded buffer visualizes as a circular queue with fill level indicator
Producers block when buffer is full, consumers block when buffer is empty
Semaphore/condition variable states displayed alongside the simulation

title: Dining Philosophers (naive vs ordered)
description: Build a simulation of the Dining Philosophers problem comparing the naive approach (deadlock-prone) with the resource ordering solution.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 philosophers and 5 forks visualized in a circular arrangement
Naive mode can demonstrate deadlock when all philosophers pick up left fork simultaneously
Ordered solution prevents deadlock by ordering fork acquisition

title: ConcurrencyModule with all 3 demos
description: Create the ConcurrencyModule shell with tab navigation for Race Condition, Producer-Consumer, and Dining Philosophers demos.
acceptanceCriteria: Module renders with tab selector for all 3 demos
Each tab lazy-loads the corresponding demo component
Module registered in the app router and accessible from navigation

title: Thread lifecycle state machine visualization
description: Build a state machine visualization of the thread lifecycle showing all 7 states (New, Ready, Running, Waiting, Timed Waiting, Blocked, Terminated) with transitions triggered by user actions.
acceptanceCriteria: State machine diagram shows all 7 thread states as nodes with labeled transitions
Active state is highlighted; users can trigger transitions via buttons (start, sleep, wait, notify, etc.)
Each transition animates with an explanation of what triggered it
Thread info panel shows current state, wait reason, and time in state

title: Async/Await event loop visualization
description: Build a Modern Loupe-inspired event loop visualization showing the call stack, microtask queue, macrotask queue, Web APIs area, and render pipeline, animating how async/await code executes.
acceptanceCriteria: Call stack panel shows function frames being pushed and popped
Microtask queue (Promise callbacks) and macrotask queue (setTimeout, etc.) rendered separately
Web APIs area shows pending timers and fetch requests with countdown
Step-by-step execution mode highlights which queue is being drained and why

title: Go goroutine visualization (channels, select, WaitGroup)
description: Build a visualization of Go concurrency primitives showing goroutines as animated entities communicating through channels, with select statement multiplexing and WaitGroup synchronization.
acceptanceCriteria: Goroutines render as colored circles with state indicators (running, blocked, waiting)
Channels visualize as pipes with buffered slots showing data items flowing through
Select statement shows multiple channel cases with the selected case highlighted
WaitGroup counter and Done/Wait calls visualized with barrier synchronization

title: Deadlock detection visualization in concurrency context
description: Build a deadlock detection visualization in the concurrency module context, showing threads holding and waiting for locks, with automatic cycle detection and highlighting.
acceptanceCriteria: Threads and locks render as a wait-for graph with directed edges
Deadlock cycle is automatically detected and highlighted in red
Users can create deadlock scenarios by assigning lock holdings and wait requests
Resolution suggestions offered (kill thread, preempt lock, timeout)

title: Custom code input for event loop visualization
description: Add a code editor input to the event loop visualization where users can type or paste JavaScript async code and see it animate through the event loop step by step.
acceptanceCriteria: Code editor accepts JavaScript with syntax highlighting (Monaco or CodeMirror)
Parser extracts setTimeout, Promise, fetch, async/await, and queueMicrotask calls
Parsed code drives the event loop visualization with accurate queue placement
Preset code examples available for common patterns (Promise chaining, async/await, mixed)

title: Race condition histogram (run 1000 times, show outcome distribution)
description: Build a statistical race condition demo that runs a simulated race condition 1000 times and displays a histogram of the final counter values, illustrating the non-deterministic outcome distribution.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation runs 1000 iterations of the unsafe increment with configurable thread count
Histogram chart displays the distribution of final counter values
Expected (correct) value marked on the histogram for comparison
Statistics panel shows mean, median, standard deviation, and min/max outcomes

title: SecurityModule shell + topic selector
description: Create the top-level SecurityModule component with a topic selector supporting OAuth 2.0, JWT, AES Encryption, Diffie-Hellman, and HTTPS Flow topics.
acceptanceCriteria: SecurityModule renders with a tab/selector for all 5 topics
Selecting a topic lazy-loads the corresponding sub-module
Active topic persists in URL query parameter for shareability
Module registered in the app router and accessible from navigation

title: Security type definitions
description: Define all TypeScript types for the security module: OAuthFlow (steps, parties, tokens), JWTToken (header, payload, signature), AESState (4x4 matrix per round), and DHParams (prime, generator, keys).
acceptanceCriteria: OAuthFlow type models multi-party sequence with request/response at each step
JWTToken type includes typed header (alg, typ), payload (claims), and signature fields
AESState type represents the 4x4 byte matrix at each round with SubBytes/ShiftRows/MixColumns transforms
DHParams type includes prime p, generator g, private keys, public keys, and shared secret

title: Security Zustand store
description: Create the Zustand store for the security module managing OAuth flow state, JWT editor content, AES round states, DH parameters, and HTTPS flow progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Store exposes slices for each security topic (OAuth, JWT, AES, DH, HTTPS)
OAuth slice tracks current flow step, party states, and token values
JWT slice manages header, payload, secret, and computed signature
AES slice tracks the 4x4 state matrix for each of the 10 rounds

title: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code + PKCE flow simulator
description: Implement the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE as a step-by-step simulator, modeling all parties (Client, Authorization Server, Resource Server, User Agent) and HTTP exchanges.
acceptanceCriteria: Flow models all steps: authorization request, user consent, auth code redirect, token exchange, resource access
PKCE adds code_verifier generation, code_challenge computation, and verification at token exchange
Each step records the full HTTP request and response (headers, body, query params)
Flow state machine prevents skipping steps and validates correct sequencing

title: PKCE implementation (code verifier/challenge with Web Crypto)
description: Implement PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) using the Web Crypto API to generate a cryptographically random code_verifier and compute the S256 code_challenge.
acceptanceCriteria: Code verifier generated as a 43-128 character URL-safe random string using crypto.getRandomValues
Code challenge computed as base64url(SHA-256(code_verifier)) using Web Crypto API
Both plain and S256 challenge methods supported
Generated values match the RFC 7636 specification format

title: OAuthFlowVisualizer (multi-party sequence with HTTP detail)
description: Build a multi-party sequence diagram visualization for OAuth flows showing Client, Auth Server, Resource Server, and User Agent as swim lanes with HTTP request/response detail panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Four party lanes (Client, Auth Server, Resource Server, User Agent) with labeled arrows between them
Each step is clickable and reveals the full HTTP request/response details in a side panel
Current step is highlighted with previous steps grayed and future steps dimmed
Step-through controls (forward, backward, play, reset) drive the flow animation

title: JWT encode/decode engine (Web Crypto API HMAC-SHA256)
description: Implement a JWT encode/decode engine using the Web Crypto API for HMAC-SHA256 signing, supporting header/payload JSON editing, base64url encoding, and signature verification.
acceptanceCriteria: Encoding: takes header JSON, payload JSON, and secret; produces a valid 3-part JWT string
Decoding: splits a JWT string and decodes each base64url part to JSON
Signature computation uses Web Crypto API HMAC-SHA256 (not a polyfill)
Verification: recomputes signature and reports valid/invalid match

title: JWTPlayground (3-color header/payload/signature, live edit)
description: Build an interactive JWT playground similar to jwt.io with three color-coded sections (header=red, payload=purple, signature=blue), live editing, and real-time encode/decode.
acceptanceCriteria: JWT string displayed with 3 color-coded dot-separated sections
Editing the header or payload JSON instantly updates the encoded JWT and signature
Editing the encoded JWT string instantly decodes and updates the JSON panels
Secret input field updates the signature in real time; verification status indicator shown

title: JWT validation flow visualization
description: Build a step-by-step visualization of the JWT validation process: parse token, decode header, decode payload, check expiration, verify signature, and validate claims.
acceptanceCriteria: Flow shows each validation step as a sequential pipeline with pass/fail indicators
Failed steps highlight in red with a description of the failure reason
Expiration check compares exp claim against current time with visual timeline
Signature verification step shows the recomputed vs. provided signature comparison

title: JWT attack demos (none algorithm, replay, confusion)
description: Build interactive demonstrations of common JWT attacks: none algorithm bypass, token replay, and algorithm confusion (RS256 to HS256), showing how each attack works and how to prevent it.
acceptanceCriteria: None algorithm demo shows crafting a token with alg:none and how the server should reject it
Replay attack demo shows capturing and reusing a token with explanation of jti/exp mitigations
Algorithm confusion demo shows using the public key as HMAC secret and how to prevent it
Each demo includes a mitigation section explaining the defense

title: AES-128 engine (SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey, key expansion)
description: Implement the AES-128 encryption algorithm with all four round operations (SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey) and key expansion, recording the 4x4 state matrix at each step for visualization.
acceptanceCriteria: SubBytes correctly applies S-box substitution to each byte of the state matrix
ShiftRows performs the correct cyclic left shifts on each row
MixColumns performs GF(2^8) matrix multiplication on each column
Key expansion generates all 11 round keys from the initial 128-bit key

title: AES S-box + GF(2^8) multiplication utilities
description: Implement the AES S-box lookup table (and its inverse) and Galois Field GF(2^8) multiplication routines needed by MixColumns.
acceptanceCriteria: S-box and inverse S-box lookup tables match the AES specification exactly
GF(2^8) multiplication correctly handles the irreducible polynomial x^8 + x^4 + x^3 + x + 1
Multiplication routines support multiply by 2 (xtime) and arbitrary multiplication via addition
All utilities are pure functions with no side effects

title: AESRoundVisualizer (4x4 matrix animation, all 10 rounds)
description: Build a visualization of AES encryption showing the 4x4 state matrix being transformed through all 10 rounds, with animated SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, and AddRoundKey operations.
acceptanceCriteria: 4x4 matrix displayed with hex byte values, color-coded to show changes per operation
Each operation (SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, AddRoundKey) animates individually within a round
Round navigation allows jumping to any round or stepping through sequentially
Key expansion schedule displayed alongside showing the round key for each round

title: Diffie-Hellman simulation (BigInt, paint analogy)
description: Implement the Diffie-Hellman key exchange simulation using JavaScript BigInt for modular exponentiation, alongside a paint-mixing color analogy for intuitive understanding.
acceptanceCriteria: Mathematical simulation uses BigInt for modular exponentiation (g^a mod p)
Paint analogy maps prime to a shared color, private keys to secret colors, and mixing to modular exponentiation
Both Alice and Bob arrive at the same shared secret independently
Eve (eavesdropper) can see public values but cannot compute the shared secret without discrete log

title: DiffieHellmanVisualizer (dual view: paint + math, with Eve)
description: Build a dual-view visualization of Diffie-Hellman key exchange with a paint-mixing analogy on one side and the mathematical operations on the other, including Eve the eavesdropper.
acceptanceCriteria: Split view shows paint-mixing analogy (left) and mathematical BigInt computations (right)
Alice and Bob columns show private key, public key computation, and shared secret derivation
Eve column in the middle shows intercepted public values and inability to derive the shared secret
Step-through animation synchronizes both views with explanatory text at each step

title: HTTPS full flow engine (DNS to TCP to TLS to HTTP to Close)
description: Implement a simulation engine for the complete HTTPS connection lifecycle: DNS resolution, TCP 3-way handshake, TLS 1.3 handshake (ClientHello, ServerHello, key exchange, Finished), HTTP request/response, and connection close.
acceptanceCriteria: DNS resolution step shows domain-to-IP lookup with cache check
TCP handshake models SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK three-way exchange
TLS 1.3 handshake includes ClientHello (supported ciphers), ServerHello (chosen cipher), key exchange, and Finished
Each step records timing, packet details, and encryption state changes

title: HTTPSFlowVisualizer (end-to-end timeline)
description: Build a timeline visualization of the complete HTTPS connection flow showing Client and Server as two columns with packets flowing between them, annotated with timing and encryption details.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-column layout (Client and Server) with animated packet arrows between them
Timeline shows DNS, TCP handshake, TLS handshake, HTTP exchange, and close phases with distinct colors
Clicking a packet shows detailed headers, cipher info, and encryption state
Cumulative latency shown on the timeline axis; each phase duration labeled

title: OAuth Client Credentials + Device Auth flow variants
description: Extend the OAuth simulator with two additional grant types: Client Credentials (machine-to-machine) and Device Authorization (for input-limited devices like smart TVs).
acceptanceCriteria: Client Credentials flow models direct token request with client_id and client_secret (no user interaction)
Device Authorization flow models device code request, user verification URL display, and polling for authorization
Each flow variant uses the same OAuthFlowVisualizer with adapted party lanes and steps
Flow selector lets users switch between Authorization Code, Client Credentials, and Device Auth

title: MLDesignModule shell + mode selector
description: Create the top-level MLDesignModule component with a mode selector supporting Neural Network Playground, CNN Visualization, Loss Landscape, ML Pipeline Builder, and ML Serving Patterns modes.
acceptanceCriteria: MLDesignModule renders with a tab/selector for all 5 modes
Selecting a mode lazy-loads the corresponding sub-module
Active mode persists in URL query parameter for shareability
Module registered in the app router and accessible from navigation

title: ML type definitions
description: Define all TypeScript types for the ML module: NeuralNetConfig (layers, learning rate, optimizer), Layer (type, neurons, activation), TrainingState (epoch, loss, accuracy), and PipelineStage (type, config, connections).
acceptanceCriteria: NeuralNetConfig type includes layer definitions, learning rate, optimizer choice, batch size, and epochs
Layer type uses discriminated union for Dense, Conv2D, Dropout, BatchNorm with layer-specific params
TrainingState type tracks epoch, batch, loss history, accuracy history, and training status
PipelineStage type models data ingestion, feature engineering, training, evaluation, and serving stages

title: ML Zustand store
description: Create the Zustand store for the ML module managing neural network configuration, training state, dataset, decision boundary, pipeline stages, and serving patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Store exposes slices for neural network config, training state, dataset, and pipeline
Neural network slice manages layers, learning rate, optimizer, and activation functions
Training state slice tracks loss/accuracy history, current epoch, and training status (idle/running/paused)
Pipeline slice manages stages, connections, and template selection

title: Dataset generators (circle, XOR, Gaussian, spiral, custom)
description: Implement 2D dataset generators for neural network training: concentric circles, XOR pattern, Gaussian clusters, spiral arms, and a custom point-drawing mode where users click to place points.
acceptanceCriteria: Each generator produces an array of {x, y, label} points with configurable count and noise level
Circle generator creates two concentric rings of different classes
Spiral generator creates interleaved spiral arms (configurable number of arms)
Custom mode lets users click on a 2D canvas to place points with class toggle

title: Neural network core: forward pass + backpropagation
description: Implement a from-scratch neural network engine with forward pass (matrix multiplication + activation) and backpropagation (gradient computation + weight update) for fully connected layers.
acceptanceCriteria: Forward pass correctly computes activations through all layers using matrix multiplication
Backpropagation computes gradients via chain rule for all weights and biases
Weight update applies gradients scaled by learning rate
Engine supports configurable layer sizes, activation functions, and loss functions

title: Activation functions (ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh, Leaky ReLU, Softmax)
description: Implement activation functions and their derivatives: ReLU, Sigmoid, Tanh, Leaky ReLU, and Softmax, as pure functions operating on number arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: Each activation function correctly computes output values for scalar and array inputs
Each activation function provides its derivative for backpropagation
Softmax is numerically stable (subtracts max before exponentiation)
Functions are pure with no side effects and fully tested against known values

title: Loss functions (MSE, Cross-Entropy)
description: Implement loss functions and their derivatives: Mean Squared Error for regression and Binary/Categorical Cross-Entropy for classification.
acceptanceCriteria: MSE correctly computes mean of squared differences between predicted and actual values
Cross-Entropy correctly computes log loss with numerical stability (clipping near 0 and 1)
Each loss function provides its derivative for backpropagation
Functions handle edge cases (zero predictions, perfect predictions) without NaN

title: Optimizers (SGD, Adam, RMSProp)
description: Implement optimization algorithms: vanilla SGD with optional momentum, Adam (with beta1, beta2, epsilon), and RMSProp, each maintaining their own state (velocity, moving averages).
acceptanceCriteria: SGD correctly updates weights with learning rate and optional momentum
Adam correctly maintains first and second moment estimates with bias correction
RMSProp correctly maintains moving average of squared gradients
Each optimizer initializes and maintains its state per parameter independently

title: Web Worker for training loop (Comlink)
description: Move the neural network training loop into a Web Worker using Comlink for a clean async API, preventing UI blocking during training.
acceptanceCriteria: Training loop runs in a dedicated Web Worker with Comlink proxy
Main thread receives progress updates (epoch, loss, accuracy) via callback/observable
Training can be paused, resumed, and stopped from the main thread
Worker handles batch processing and reports per-epoch metrics without blocking UI

title: NeuralNetPlayground canvas (network architecture + data viz)
description: Build the main playground canvas with a split view: left side shows the neural network architecture as an interactive node graph, right side shows the 2D dataset with decision boundary overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Network architecture renders layers as columns of neuron circles with connection lines
Connection line thickness/color represents weight magnitude/sign
Data visualization shows training points colored by class with decision boundary heatmap
Training controls (play, pause, reset, step) positioned between the two views

title: NeuralNetLayer + NeuronNode visualization
description: Build reusable components for visualizing individual neural network layers and neurons, with hover tooltips showing weights, bias, and activation values.
acceptanceCriteria: NeuralNetLayer renders a vertical column of NeuronNode circles with layer label
NeuronNode shows activation value as fill color intensity (blue for negative, orange for positive)
Hovering a neuron shows a tooltip with its weights, bias, and current activation value
Connection lines between layers color-code by weight value (red negative, blue positive)

title: DecisionBoundary 2D heatmap
description: Build a 2D heatmap visualization that renders the neural network's decision boundary by evaluating the network on a dense grid of points and mapping output to colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Dense grid of evaluation points (e.g., 100x100) covers the data space
Network output at each grid point is mapped to a color (class A color to class B color gradient)
Heatmap updates during training at configurable intervals (every N epochs)
Training data points are overlaid on top of the heatmap with class-colored markers

title: TrainingHistoryChart (loss/accuracy over epochs)
description: Build a line chart component that plots training loss and accuracy over epochs, updating in real time during training.
acceptanceCriteria: Dual-axis line chart shows loss (left axis) and accuracy (right axis) over epochs
Chart updates in real time as new epoch metrics arrive from the training worker
Zoom and pan controls allow inspecting specific epoch ranges
Legend toggles for showing/hiding individual metrics

title: CNN layer forward pass + visualization
description: Implement a Conv2D forward pass (convolution with configurable kernels, stride, padding) and build a visualization showing input, kernel, and output feature maps with sliding window animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Conv2D forward pass correctly computes convolution with configurable kernel size, stride, and padding
Visualization shows input feature map, kernel weights, and output feature map as grids
Sliding window animation highlights the receptive field on input and the computed output cell
Multiple kernels produce multiple output channels displayed side by side

title: Dropout + Batch Normalization visualization
description: Implement Dropout (randomly zeroing neurons during training) and Batch Normalization (normalizing layer inputs) with visualizations showing their effects on neuron activations.
acceptanceCriteria: Dropout visualization shows randomly grayed-out neurons during training and all active during inference
Batch Normalization shows activation distribution before and after normalization as histograms
Dropout rate is configurable and visually reflects the percentage of dropped neurons
Toggle between training and inference modes to show the behavioral difference

title: Loss landscape 3D surface plot (canvas isometric)
description: Build a 3D loss landscape visualization as an isometric surface plot on an HTML canvas, showing the loss function surface over a 2D parameter slice with the optimization trajectory plotted as a path.
acceptanceCriteria: Isometric 3D surface renders the loss function over a 2D grid of parameter values
Surface color maps from blue (low loss) to red (high loss)
Optimization trajectory shows the path of gradient descent as a line on the surface
Camera angle can be rotated by dragging to inspect the landscape from different views

title: NeuralNetConfig sidebar panel
description: Build a configuration sidebar for the neural network playground allowing users to add/remove layers, set neuron counts, choose activation functions, select optimizer, and configure learning rate.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows the layer stack with add/remove buttons and drag-to-reorder
Each layer row has controls for neuron count and activation function selection
Global settings section for learning rate, optimizer, batch size, and epoch count
Dataset selector dropdown switches between available dataset generators

title: ML Pipeline builder (React Flow, drag-drop stages)
description: Build an ML pipeline builder using React Flow where users drag-and-drop pipeline stages (Data Ingestion, Feature Engineering, Training, Evaluation, Serving) onto a canvas and connect them.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline stages available as draggable items in a side palette
Dropped stages create React Flow nodes with stage-specific configuration panels
Edges between stages represent data flow with type annotations (DataFrame, Model, Metrics)
Pipeline validates connectivity (single source, no cycles, required stages present)

title: Pipeline stage nodes + data flow edges
description: Build custom React Flow nodes for each ML pipeline stage (Data Source, Feature Store, Training, Evaluation, Model Registry, Serving) and typed edges representing data flow between them.
acceptanceCriteria: Each stage type has a distinct icon and color scheme for quick identification
Stage nodes show a summary of their configuration (e.g., model type, feature list)
Data flow edges display the data type being passed (raw data, features, model artifact, metrics)
Clicking a stage node opens a configuration panel specific to that stage type

title: Pipeline templates (Spotify, TikTok)
description: Create pre-built ML pipeline templates modeled after real-world systems (Spotify recommendation pipeline, TikTok content ranking pipeline) that users can load and explore.
acceptanceCriteria: Spotify template includes user feature extraction, collaborative filtering, content-based filtering, and blended ranking
TikTok template includes content understanding, user modeling, candidate generation, and ranking with engagement prediction
Each template loads a pre-configured pipeline with all stages and connections
Templates include documentation panels explaining the architecture decisions

title: Feature Store architecture visualization
description: Build a visualization of a Feature Store architecture showing offline (batch) and online (real-time) feature pipelines, feature registry, and serving infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagram shows batch and streaming data sources feeding into feature computation
Offline store (data warehouse) and online store (key-value cache) are clearly depicted
Feature registry component shows feature metadata, versioning, and lineage
Data flow arrows show how features move from computation to storage to serving

title: Model Serving patterns visualization (A/B, canary, shadow, ensemble)
description: Build visualizations of 4 model serving patterns: A/B testing (traffic splitting), canary deployment (gradual rollout), shadow mode (parallel inference), and ensemble serving (multi-model aggregation).
acceptanceCriteria: A/B testing visualization shows traffic router splitting requests to model A and model B with percentage controls
Canary visualization shows gradual traffic shift from old model to new model with rollback trigger
Shadow mode shows requests going to both production and shadow models with comparison panel
Ensemble visualization shows multiple models contributing to a final aggregated prediction

title: A/B Testing system (user assignment, significance calculator)
description: Build an interactive A/B testing system simulator with user assignment to variants, metric collection, and a statistical significance calculator using chi-squared or z-test.
acceptanceCriteria: User assignment engine supports random split with configurable variant weights
Metrics collection tracks conversion rate, click-through rate, or custom metric per variant
Statistical significance calculator computes p-value and confidence interval
Visualization shows running experiment with sample size, metric values, and significance indicator

title: Multi-armed bandit visualization (epsilon-greedy, UCB1, Thompson)
description: Build a multi-armed bandit simulation and visualization supporting three strategies: epsilon-greedy, UCB1 (Upper Confidence Bound), and Thompson Sampling, showing arm selection and reward accumulation over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Bandit simulation supports configurable number of arms with hidden reward probabilities
Epsilon-greedy strategy explores with probability epsilon and exploits with 1-epsilon
UCB1 strategy balances exploration and exploitation using confidence bounds
Thompson Sampling uses Beta distribution posterior with animated probability density curves
